7710cc5 refactor(config): app URL resolution in synced appUrl.ts (Vercel auto + override) [90m(03/06/2026)[0m
  Projects used to silently inherit a HARDCODED app-template-ai.vercel.app default
  when they forgot to set a URL — shipping links at the wrong domain. Fix it
  without breaking the fleet, AND make the fix actually reach child projects.
  
  Key: the resolution now lives in src/server/template/appUrl.ts (template-owned,
  SYNCED), NOT in src/app.config.js (project-owned, NOT synced — logic there never
  reaches children). Every template link-builder reads getAppUrl()/requireAppUrl().
  
  Resolution (in appUrl.ts):
    1. NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL            — explicit override (custom domain / pin)
    2. VERCEL_PROJECT_PRODUCTION_URL  — Vercel auto, per-project correct (zero-
                                        config default; prod AND preview)
    3. dev → http://localhost:3000
    4. else null → requireAppUrl() throws (only off Vercel, unconfigured)
  No hardcoded fallback domain (the bug). appUrl is server-only (no client usage),
  so server-only VERCEL_PROJECT_PRODUCTION_URL suffices.
  
  - All consumers route through appUrl.ts: telegram getBaseUrl, passkey enroll,
    login-approval, reset, agent links, and webauthn rpID/origin (was the last
    direct appConfig.appUrl reader).
  - src/app.config.js keeps `appUrl` only as a project-level mirror (not synced,
    not read by template code).
  - Existing children need NO migration — appUrl.ts + its per-project Vercel
    default sync in and just work.
  - `yarn set-app-url <url>` sets the OVERRIDE on Vercel (all envs); --local for
    .env.local.
  - Docs: .env.example, critical-deployment-issues.md, /init-project, /sync-template
    reframed to "auto on Vercel, override only for custom domains".
  
  yarn checks green.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

0ed175c feat(auth): passkey step-up guard for sensitive pages [90m(03/06/2026)[0m
  Gate any page/section behind a fresh passkey assertion (re-auth) — the user is
  logged in but must confirm device possession (Face ID / Touch ID / device PIN)
  before sensitive content renders. On failure: a generic lock screen with a
  "Try again" button. Stays unlocked for a TTL (default 5 min, in-memory).
  
  Two ways to use it:
  - Route-level (preferred): `{ component, requirePasskey: true }` in the routes
    config — the router resolves it into context and RoutePasskeyGuard (mounted
    once in _app.tsx) wraps the page. Router never imports auth UI (no cycle).
  - Component: <PasskeyGuard guardKey=…> for guarding a section.
  
  - Server: auth/passkey/step-up/options + verify (assertion restricted to the
    user's own credentials, stateless verify → { verified, verifiedAt }).
  - Client: usePasskeyStepUp hook + usePasskeyGuardStore (in-memory, keyed by
    guardKey; never persisted so it re-prompts after reload).
  - Example: /sensitive (SensitiveExample) gated via route-level requirePasskey;
    added to the project menu.
  - Docs: passwordless-passkeys.md + routing-guidelines route-options table.
  
  yarn checks green.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

5f38c92 feat(rpc): device-auth via passkey for RPC connections (passkey mode) [90m(03/06/2026)[0m
  In passkey mode, opening an RPC connection now requires a passkey assertion on
  a registered device instead of Telegram admin approval — self-service, and it
  proves the connection comes from a device registered to the user. Admin-only,
  as before. Password mode keeps the existing Telegram flow unchanged (branched
  on AUTH_MODE).
  
  Flow: connect-options (assertion restricted to the user's registered passkeys
  + daemon liveness precheck) → Face ID → connect-verify (verify against the
  stored credential → open an ALREADY-APPROVED connection bound to that
  credentialId). The gate itself is unchanged — it only checks an approved,
  unexpired token, so it doesn't care how the row was approved.
  
  - ceremonies.buildAuthenticationOptions() gains optional allowCredentials.
  - rpc_connections rows gain approvalMethod ('telegram'|'passkey') + credentialId;
    new createApprovedRpcConnection() inserts an approved row directly.
  - New endpoints admin/rpc-connections/connect-options + connect-verify; client
    apiRpcConnectOptions/Verify; useConnectRpc branches on useAuthMode.
  - Indicator + expiry-warning copy made mode-aware ("verify this device").
  - Docs: rpc-connection-gate.md (two approval paths) + passwordless-passkeys.md.
  
  yarn checks green.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

e38035c feat(auth): admin-generated passkey enrollment links [90m(02/06/2026)[0m
  Build the universal token-authenticated enrollment flow and deliver the link
  via the admin (SES email is deferred). When email lands later it just sends
  the SAME /enroll-passkey?token= URL — no flow change.
  
  Backend:
  - auth/passkey/enroll/options + /verify — PUBLIC, authorized by a one-time
    enrollment token (not a session), so a user with no passkey yet can register
    their first device. Verify stores the credential, consumes the token, and
    issues the JWT session for approved users (honors the admin-approval gate).
    Reuses the enrollment_tokens collection + ceremony wrappers.
  - admin/users/generate-passkey-link (admin-gated) — invalidates prior links,
    mints a fresh 1h single-use token, returns ${appUrl}/enroll-passkey?token=…
  - countCredentialsByUser() aggregate; AdminUserSummary gains approvalStatus +
    passkeyCount (no N+1).
  
  Frontend:
  - /admin/users page (new "Users" nav item): lists users w/ approval status +
    passkey count; per-user "Generate passkey link" → dialog w/ copy + expiry +
    regenerate.
  - /enroll-passkey landing (public, full-screen): reads ?token=, runs the
    WebAuthn ceremony, drops approved users in signed-in; friendly states for
    expired/missing token + unsupported browser.
  
  Security: token is bearer (like password-reset) — single-use, 1h TTL, one
  active per user. yarn checks green.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

37199e0 fix(auth): accept any localhost port for passkey dev origin [90m(02/06/2026)[0m
  Next dev hops to 3001/3002/... when 3000 is busy, but the WebAuthn

  range of localhost + 127.0.0.1 ports in dev, with WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN as an

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

d64e81f feat(auth): passwordless passkeys — Phase 2 (discoverable login) [90m(01/06/2026)[0m
  Implements the "just tap" passkey login flow behind AUTH_MODE=passkey.
  Password mode remains the default; all downstream session plumbing
  (JWT cookie, instant-boot, MCP/SDK bearer) is unchanged.
  
  - ceremonies.ts: add buildAuthenticationOptions() (empty allowCredentials
    → discoverable) and verifyAuthentication() (base64url public key decode,
    counter advancement)
  - handlers/passkey/loginOptions.ts: public endpoint — generates a
    single-use auth challenge, stores it, returns challengeId for correlation
  - handlers/passkey/loginVerify.ts: public endpoint — consumes challenge,
    looks up credential by assertion id, applies admin-approval gate,
    verifies assertion, advances counter, issues same JWT cookie + recordSession
    as password login; generic error on any failure (no passkey enumeration)
  - auth/index.ts + server.ts + types.ts + client.ts: wire new endpoints
    into registry, export API name constants, shared types, and API client fns
  - passkeyHooks.ts: usePasskeyLogin() — full ceremony (login-options →
    startAuthentication → login-verify → setValidatedUser/setUserHint);
    handles ceremony-aborted and offline edge cases
  - LoginForm.tsx: "Sign in with a passkey" button shown only when
    authMode === 'passkey' + WebAuthn supported + sign-in tab active
  - plan updated to Phase 2 complete
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

882b574 feat(auth): passwordless passkeys — Phase 0 (foundation) + Phase 1 (enroll) [90m(01/06/2026)[0m
  Lay the groundwork for passwordless WebAuthn auth and ship the first
  user-facing slice. Everything is additive and dormant behind AUTH_MODE
  (default 'password'), so synced-but-unmigrated child projects keep running
  today's bcrypt flow unchanged. Passkey *login* is not wired yet (Phase 2).
  
  Phase 0 — foundation:
  - AUTH_MODE master switch (src/apis/template/auth/authMode.ts), surfaced on
    /me so the unauthenticated login UI learns the mode from preflight.
  - WebAuthn RP config (src/server/template/webauthn/config.ts): dev=localhost,
    prod=WEBAUTHN_RP_ID/appUrl host; documents the preview-URL gotcha.
  - New template collections: credentials (unique credentialId), single-use
    webauthn_challenges (TTL), enrollment_tokens (reset-token clone).
  - Deps: @simplewebauthn/server + @simplewebauthn/browser.
  - .env.example documents AUTH_MODE / WEBAUTHN_RP_ID / WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN.
  
  Phase 1 — enroll a passkey for logged-in users (registration ceremony only):
  - Ceremony wrappers (buildRegistrationOptions w/ discoverable residentKey,
    verifyRegistration) applying RP config centrally.
  - Endpoints auth/passkey/{register-options,register-verify,list,delete},
    per-handler userId auth; single-use, user-bound challenges.
  - Client hooks (usePasskeys/useAddPasskey/useDeletePasskey) + PasskeysSection
    on Profile (add / list / remove-with-confirm), gated on browser support.
  
  Email deliverability (SES) remains the one developer-action prerequisite
  before any cutover. yarn checks green.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

c3c3459 docs(plan): passwordless passkeys (WebAuthn) — approved design [90m(01/06/2026)[0m
  Durable plan for replacing password auth with passkeys across the
  template + child projects. Locked decisions: per-project hard cutover via
  a migration skill, discoverable "just tap" login, one universal
  email-link enrollment flow (signup = add-device = recovery = migration),
  email mandatory, NO recovery codes (admin-assisted backstop), and an
  AUTH_MODE env flag (default password) so synced-but-unmigrated projects
  keep working on password auth. Includes the credentials/challenge/
  enrollment-token model, the two flows, build order, and risks.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

11138df fix(ai): lenient JSON parsing for Claude Code SDK responses [90m(01/06/2026)[0m

a7b9339 fix(agent): make answer recap render-safe against legacy/cached shapes [90m(30/05/2026)[0m
  Server-side normalization didn't help here because the client renders
  from its persisted React Query cache (localStorage), which still holds
  old-shape answers (string[] instead of {selected, other}). The widget
  now coerces each answer via asAnswer() and guards missing `questions`,
  so it can never crash on an unexpected shape regardless of cache age.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

2651c6a fix(agent): tolerate legacy ask_user answer shapes [90m(30/05/2026)[0m
  Question rows answered before the schema evolved store `answers` as
  `string[][]` (or are missing `questions`), which crashed AnsweredRecap
  when it read `answer.selected` on a bare array. toQuestionClient now
  normalizes legacy `questions`/`answers` into the current
  {selected, other} shape at the wire boundary, so the client always gets
  a valid shape (old rows self-clear via the 1-day TTL).
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

df9b347 feat(agent): ask_user "Other…" free-text answers [90m(30/05/2026)[0m
  Each question now (by default) accepts a free-text "Other" answer the
  agent receives separately as `other`. It's a distinct channel: the user
  can fill it INSTEAD of an option (a non-empty Other waives the minimum-
  selection requirement) or ALONGSIDE a chosen option (e.g. pick option 1
  and add a comment). Set allowOther:false to force a closed choice.
  
  - types/collection: AgentQuestionAnswer { selected, other? }; per-question
    allowOther flag; validateAnswer enforces max + waives min when Other set.
  - ask_user tool: allowOther in schema (default true), returns
    { responses: [{ question, selected, other? }] }.
  - widget: a dashed "Other" input under the options, highlighted when
    filled; recap + event summary include the Other text.
  - API/hooks/wiring switch answers to AgentQuestionAnswer[].
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

  - On submit, the answered question renders a compact recap ("Your

bdad140 feat(agent): ask_user supports multiple questions + single/multi choice [90m(30/05/2026)[0m
  Reshapes the human-in-the-loop tool to mirror the native
  AskUserQuestion schema: a `questions[]` batch where each question is
  single-choice or multiSelect and each option is `{label, description}`.
  Covers all four modes: single-choice / multi-choice × single question /
  multiple questions, answered together with one Submit.
  
  - agentQuestions collection stores a question batch + per-question
    answers (string[][]); answerQuestion validates each sub-question
    against its own options + min/max bounds.
  - ask_user tool: questions[] input, returns { responses: [{question,
    selected}] }; per-question normalization of options/multiSelect/bounds.
  - Widget renders all questions with one Submit, option descriptions,
    radio (single) vs check (multi) affordance, locked answered state.
  - API/hooks/wiring switch from `selected: string[]` to
    `answers: string[][]`. System prompt + eventSummary updated.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

3300da2 fix(init-project): strip template identity keys from copied env files [90m(30/05/2026)[0m
  When creating a project from the template, init-project copies .env and
  .env.local from ../app-template-ai to bootstrap shared values (MONGO_URI,
  etc.). That also copied the template developer's LOCAL_USER_ID and
  ADMIN_USER_ID: the leaked LOCAL_USER_ID pre-set process.env so
  createLocalUserAndWriteEnv early-exited and never created a fresh local
  user in the new project's database, and the dev was wrongly treated as
  admin.
  
  Strip LOCAL_USER_ID and ADMIN_USER_ID (active or commented) from each env
  file right after copying, so the new project regenerates its own id.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    `fileStorageAPI.uploadBuffer` with per-user folder. Vercel Blob
    provider hardened: `addRandomSuffix: true` (so colliding filenames
    like "image.png" don't conflict) and the double-extension bug
    (`image.png.png`) is fixed by detecting trailing extensions on the
    caller-supplied filename. Both upload methods now share a single
    private `putBuffer` helper.
  - `agentMessages` schema gains optional `attachments?` field; the
    user message persists them and the client receives them via
    `toMessageClient`.
  - `sendMessage` splits image vs non-image attachments — images flow

  - `createAgentHandler` parses + threads `userImageUrls` from RPC args.
  - Claude Code adapter: when images are present, switches from the
    string-prompt form to `AsyncIterable<SDKUserMessage>`, fetches each
    URL server-side, base64-encodes, and emits content blocks with

    file becomes a slot, flips through 'uploading' → 'uploaded' /

96f66a5 feat(agent): expose project APIs as agent tools via co-located ApiMeta [90m(25/05/2026)[0m
  Adds an opt-in mechanism for projects to surface any API endpoint to
  the AI agent as a typed tool — no bespoke tool authoring required.
  
  Infrastructure
  - `ApiMeta` (`src/apis/types.ts`): per-handler metadata —
    description, Zod inputSchema, agentExposed (default-deny),
    optional `mutates` flag.
  - `defineApiMeta<TRequest>()`: curried identity helper that enforces
    at compile time that the Zod schema infers a type assignable to
    TRequest. Mismatches surface as a TS error referencing the
    sentinel field `_SCHEMA_MISMATCH_` — the field name is the
    diagnostic. Calling without a generic skips the check.
  - `ApiHandlersWithMeta` registry shape preserves optional `meta` per
    entry; `mergeApiHandlers` carries it through. Backwards-compatible
    with template handlers that have no meta.
  - `buildAgentToolsFromApis({ handlers, filter? })`: walks the merged
    registry, emits one `AgenticTool` per `agentExposed: true` entry.
    Synthesizes an `ApiHandlerContext` with `userId` from the agent's
    tool context, `isAdmin: false` unconditional, inert cookie ops.
    Re-validates args against Zod before invoking the handler.
    Normalizes soft-error envelopes ({ error }) into ToolResult.
  - Collision guard: two API names that collapse to the same tool
    name throw at build time with both source API names in the error.
  
  Demo-agent wiring
  - handler.ts and codex-mcp-server.ts both call
    `buildAgentToolsFromApis(apiHandlers)` and concat with the
    hand-rolled tools. New opt-ins land automatically next reload.
  
  POC opt-ins (4 todos APIs)
  - todos/getTodos (read, no params)
  - todos/getTodo (read, by id)
  - todos/createTodo (mutates; intentionally omits `_id` — used only
    for offline-queue idempotency, agent has no concept of retry IDs)
  - todos/updateTodo (mutates; can mark done/undone)
  
  Docs
  - docs/template/agent-api-tools.md: architecture, security model
    (5 layered defenses), naming, mutation semantics, the session-
    resume gotcha (cached Claude sessions don't see newly-added
    tools — start a new conversation), step-by-step "add a new API"
    guide, description style conventions. Indexed into CLAUDE.md via
    the doc-frontmatter pipeline.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

36b70d3 fix(agentic): harden trace pipeline, MCP tool errors, and resume retry [90m(23/05/2026)[0m
  - appendTrace now upserts with $setOnInsert so calling startTrace
    upstream is optional. Projects that open the trace on the API layer
    (Vercel sendMessage.ts) get pre-daemon events; projects that don't
    still get daemon-side events because the first append creates the
    row. Caller passes a TraceContext (userId, conversationId).
  - Memoize the agentTraces collection promise so concurrent first
    callers can't race the index init. Reset on rejection so the next
    caller retries instead of getting a permanently-rejected promise.
  - MCP tool arg validation: switch parseToolArgs to safeParse and
    return a normal ToolResult envelope (isError: true) on Zod failure
    instead of letting the error bubble to a JSON-RPC error. Models
    can now self-correct via the usual retry path.
  - Drop err.stack from the adapter.threw trace entry — stack traces
    are admin-only per CLAUDE.md and trace entries are exposed via a
    user-scoped query.
  - Narrow the resume-failure retry heuristic from /session/i to a
    pattern that matches actual "session not found / missing / invalid"
    errors. Rate limits, auth failures, and timeouts no longer trigger
    wasted retries.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

07e01aa feat(agentic): generic agentic LLM engine [90m(23/05/2026)[0m
  Adds src/server/template/agentic/ — a provider-neutral agentic LLM
  runtime that child projects can use to build domain-specific agents
  (ai-doctor, fitness-training, diet-coach, finance-manager, etc.)
  with minimal glue.
  
  Public surface (import from '@/server/template/agentic'):
  
    - createAgentHandler(config)
        Returns the daemon-side RPC handler function. Wraps the full
        lifecycle: validate args, open trace, pick adapter, run with
        retry-on-resume-failure, finalize message, close trace.
  
    - initClaudeCode(config) / initCodex(config)
        Configured AgenticAdapter instances. Drop into the handler's
        adapters list.
  
    - runCodexMcpServer({ agentName, tools, createDataContext })
        Boots the stdio MCP server the Codex adapter spawns per turn.
        Project ships a ~5-line bootstrap file that calls this.
  
    - createToolBuilder<TData>()
        Typed tool factory — Zod inputSchema flows into handler args.
  
    - AgentConversationsCollection interface
        What the project's conversations wrapper implements (3 methods:
        appendAgentEvent, finalizeAssistantMessage,
        setConversationSessionId).
  
    - AgentTraceDocument collection
        Generic trace storage (status + entries), keyed by message id,
        30-day TTL.
  
  Per-agent config is minimal: only `agentName` is required on the
  adapter config; everything else is derived by convention (Claude
  Code MCP server name = agentName, Codex mcp_servers key =
  agentName.replace('-','_'), Codex MCP server path defaults to
  src/server/project/<agentName>-agent/adapters/codex-mcp-server.ts).
  
  Deps added: @openai/codex-sdk ^0.133.0 (Codex SDK), zod ^4.0.0
  (was transitive — now explicit since the engine imports it
  directly).

d036c66 fix(vercel-webhook): await Telegram send before responding [90m(12/05/2026)[0m
  Vercel's serverless runtime freezes the function instance the moment
  the response is sent, so the original fire-and-forget pattern
  (res.status(200).json(...) then void sendTelegramNotification()) never
  actually got to make the Telegram POST — the function was killed
  mid-promise on every request.
  
  Local dev never showed this because dev mode runs as a long-lived Node
  process where background promises complete normally. Production deploys
  silently dropped every webhook event.
  
  Awaiting the send before responding adds <500ms to the response time
  and keeps us well inside Vercel's webhook timeout. If we ever do need
  to ACK fast (e.g. avoid retry storms during a Telegram outage), the
  right tool is @vercel/functions waitUntil, not fire-and-forget.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

867ba97 feat(query): add useOptimisticMutation wrapper + adopt in Todo hooks [90m(11/05/2026)[0m
  Adds `useOptimisticMutation` in `src/client/query/`: a thin wrapper around
  `useMutation` that bakes in the optimistic-only pattern documented in
  react-query-mutations.md and offline-pwa-support.md.
  
  What it bakes in:
  - Cancel + snapshot + rollback for every key declared in `affectedKeys`
  - `errorToast` on failure (with override or suppression via `errorMessage`)
  - Defensive `invalidateQueries` after rollback so a missed key self-corrects
  - `onSuccess`/`onSettled` cannot write to cache from the server response —
    the wrapper exposes `onSuccess` only for UI side effects (toast, navigate,
    log)
  - Offline path stays intact: when `apiClient.post` returns `{}`,
    `mutationFn` does not throw and `onError` does not fire, so the offline
    banner remains the feedback for the offline path
  
  Adoption is optional — plain `useMutation` still works as long as it follows
  the rules in react-query-mutations.md. The wrapper makes "doing it right"
  the default and "doing it wrong" require effort.
  
  Migrates the Todo route hooks (`useCreateTodo`, `useUpdateTodo`,
  `useDeleteTodo`) to the wrapper as the reference implementation, including
  the multi-key case (update + delete touch both list and per-item caches).
  
  New doc: `docs/template/use-optimistic-mutation.md` with API, examples,
  when-not-to-use guidance, and a hand-rolled-to-wrapper migration snippet.
  Linked from `react-query-mutations.md` as a "skip the wiring" option.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

42e7f0e docs: require visible mutation errors + rollback every key in onError [90m(11/05/2026)[0m
  Adds a load-bearing rule across react-query-mutations.md, error-handling.md,
  offline-pwa-support.md, and state-management-guidelines.md: every useMutation's
  onError MUST call errorToast(message, err), roll back every cache key onMutate
  touched, and defensively invalidate the affected keys so a missed rollback key
  self-corrects on next fetch.
  
  Root cause: a child project hit an infinite-spinner bug where .mutate() was
  fire-and-forget, onError had only rollback logic, and a server-side validation
  rejection rendered with zero feedback to the user. The existing "optimistic-only"
  rule didn't explicitly mandate error surfacing — and offline-pwa-support.md's
  "only rollback in onError" bullet actively pushed devs toward the silent-failure
  pattern.
  
  Also clarifies the offline boundary: when offline, apiClient.post returns {}
  (not an error) so onError never fires — the offline banner + batch-sync alert
  are the feedback. errorToast in onError only triggers for genuine server-side
  errors, never for network unavailability.
  
  For mission-critical first-impression flows (signup, payment, onboarding), a
  toast is not enough — callers should use mutateAsync + try/catch and open a
  blocking failure dialog with copyable trace and retry actions.
  
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

beb49b0 fix(init-project): track init state via flags instead of value matching [90m(05/05/2026)[0m
  The skip-prompts check for app.config.js compared appName against a
  hardcoded constant ('App Template') that drifted out of sync with the
  actual template default ('App Template AI' in src/app.config.js). On
  projects that still had the template default, the !== comparison was
  always truthy, so init-project incorrectly marked them as already
  configured and never prompted for the project name.
  
  Replace the value-based heuristic with an explicit `init.appConfig`
  flag stored on `.template-sync.json`. Track the answer the user gave,
  not the resulting value, so the check can't drift again.
  
  - Reorder main(): runInitTemplate runs first so `.template-sync.json`
    exists when we set the flag.
  - Add legacy migration: projects whose appName already differs from
    the (correctly named) template default get the flag set
    automatically and skip prompts as before.
  - Drop the same brittle name-comparison from createManifest in favor
    of plain file-existence + a force-overwrite path for the prompt
    branch (so prompt answers actually replace stale defaults).
